name: Kedarkantha
description: Kedarkantha is India's most beloved winter trek, threading through some of the densest deodar and oak forests in Uttarakhand before opening onto pristine snow bowls. The pre-dawn summit push rewards you with a panoramic sweep of peaks — Swargarohini, Bandarpoonch, and Kala Nag — glowing in the first light. Frozen campsites, starlit skies, and the silence of deep snow make this a bucket-list Himalayan experience for beginners and veterans alike.

itinerary.title: Juda Ka Talab
itinerary.description: Trek through dense pine forests to the frozen lake.

name: Brahmatal
description: Brahmatal offers one of the finest frozen lake experiences in the Indian Himalaya, set against dramatic backdrops of Mt. Trishul (7,120 m) and Mt. Nanda Ghunti (6,309 m). The trek winds through rhododendron forests that blaze crimson in spring and threads above the snowline for most of its length in winter. The high-altitude meadows and the surreal mirrored surface of Brahmatal lake at 12,250 ft are the kind of views you'll keep returning to.

itinerary.title: Bekaltal
itinerary.description: Trek through rhododendron and oak forests.

itinerary.title: Brahmatal
itinerary.description: Trek to the high altitude lake.

itinerary.title: Summit Push
itinerary.description: Climb Brahmatal Pass for panoramic views.

name: Roopkund
description: The Roopkund trek leads to a glacial lake at 15,750 ft whose shores hold one of the greatest mysteries of the Himalaya — hundreds of ancient human skeletons, believed to be victims of a catastrophic hailstorm. The trail traverses twin bugyals of Ali and Bedni, considered the most beautiful high-altitude meadows in India, before a brutal ascent to Junargali Pass. Every step of this challenging trek is rewarded with landscapes that feel borrowed from the roof of the world.

itinerary.title: Didina
itinerary.description: Descent to the Neel Ganga river.

itinerary.title: Ali Bugyal
itinerary.description: Steep ascent into the alpine meadows.

name: Valley of Flowers
description: Tucked inside a glacially-carved valley in the Zanskar range, the Valley of Flowers National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage site that transforms into an astonishing canvas of over 500 species of alpine wildflowers during the monsoon. The trail passes through Govindghat and Ghangaria before entering the valley proper, where snow-fed streams meander through meadows blanketed in blue poppies, brahmakamal, and anemones. A rare pilgrimage for naturalists and trekkers in equal measure.

itinerary.title: The Valley
itinerary.description: Explore the majestic Valley of Flowers.

itinerary.title: Hemkund Sahib
itinerary.description: Steep ascent to the glacial lake.

name: Har Ki Dun
description: Har Ki Dun — 'Valley of Gods' — is one of the oldest inhabited trekking valleys in India, cradled by the peaks of Swargarohini and Hata. The trail follows the Tons river through charming Garhwali villages, dense silver birch forests, and vast open meadows still practising ancient barter traditions. Mythologically, this is believed to be the route the Pandavas took to heaven — and the otherworldly beauty of the valley at twilight makes that legend entirely believable.

itinerary.title: Taluka
itinerary.description: Drive to Taluka and trek to Pauni Garaat.

itinerary.title: The Valley
itinerary.description: Explore the majestic Har Ki Dun.

name: Hampta Pass
description: Hampta Pass (14,100 ft) is one of the most dramatic crossings in Himachal Pradesh, connecting the lush green Kullu Valley with the stark, moon-like landscape of the Lahaul plateau in a single day's walk. The contrast is staggering — dense alpine meadows and rushing rivers on one side, absolute arid emptiness on the other. The crossing itself is thrilling and technical in places, and the optional day trip to the emerald-green Chandratal Lake makes this trek one of the finest in the Western Himalaya.

itinerary.title: Jobra
itinerary.description: Drive from Manali and trek to Chika.

itinerary.title: The Pass
itinerary.description: Cross Hampta Pass and descend to Shea Goru.

itinerary.title: Spiti Valley
itinerary.description: Trek to Chatru and drive to Chandratal.

itinerary.title: Return
itinerary.description: Drive back to Manali via Rohtang.

name: Beas Kund
description: Beas Kund is a sacred glacial lake nestled below the Rohtang Pass at 13,054 ft, believed to be the source of the Beas River and the place where the sage Vyas composed the Mahabharata. The three-day trek from Dhundi offers sweeping views of Shitidhar, Friendship Peak, and Hanuman Tibba — a gallery of 6,000 m peaks accessible without technical climbing. Perfect for first-time high-altitude trekkers seeking big mountain drama without extreme commitment.

itinerary.title: The Glacial Lake
itinerary.description: Climb the moraine to Beas Kund.

name: Buran Ghati
description: Buran Ghati Pass (15,000 ft) offers one of the most exhilarating descents in Himalayan trekking — a near-vertical rappel down a snow wall on the far side of the pass. The approach through the Sangla Valley and Rupin basin covers dense forests, flower-strewn meadows, and high-altitude campsites with views of Kinnaur Kailash. This is a serious, physically demanding trek that rewards those who come prepared with some of the most dramatic terrain in Himachal Pradesh.

itinerary.title: Chandranahan
itinerary.description: Acclimatization walk to the alpine lake.

itinerary.title: Dhunda
itinerary.description: Climb to the base of the pass.

itinerary.title: The Ice Wall
itinerary.description: Cross Buran Ghati and rappel down.

name: Bhrigu Lake
description: Bhrigu Lake (14,100 ft) sits above the Kullu Valley and is revered as the bathing spot of the sage Bhrigu. The short but steep three-day trek passes through some of the finest high-altitude meadows in Himachal, with unobstructed views of the entire Rohtang range and the distant peaks of the Zanskar. The lake itself remains partially frozen even in peak summer, its dark waters surrounded by a crescent of snowfield — a moment of pure Himalayan serenity.

itinerary.title: The Lake
itinerary.description: Push to the frozen Bhrigu Lake.

name: Pin Parvati Pass
description: The Pin Parvati Pass (17,457 ft) is one of the highest and most challenging treks in India, crossing from the lush green Parvati Valley in Kullu to the austere Spiti desert in a high-altitude traverse that demands genuine mountaineering experience. The approach through Kheerganga and Mantalai offers cascading waterfalls and wild camping, while the descent into Pin Valley drops you into a brown, wind-carved moonscape. Reserved for experienced trekkers with technical gear and high-altitude acclimatisation.

itinerary.title: Kheerganga
itinerary.description: Trek through the dense Parvati valley.

itinerary.title: Tunda Bhuj
itinerary.description: Cross the river into higher terrain.

itinerary.title: The Traverse
itinerary.description: Cross the massive Pin Parvati glacier.

itinerary.title: Mud
itinerary.description: Arrive at the first Spiti village.

name: Goechala
description: Goechala Pass (16,207 ft) is the premier viewpoint in the entire Eastern Himalaya, placing you face-to-face with the world's third-highest mountain — Kanchenjunga (8,586 m) — at terrifyingly close range. The ten-day trek through the Kanchenjunga Conservation Area passes ancient monasteries, yak pastures, and alpine lakes en route to the pass, where sunrise colours the south face of Kanchenjunga in shades of fire. Arguably the most awe-inspiring mountain view accessible on foot in India.

itinerary.title: Tshoka
itinerary.description: Steep climb to the Tibetan settlement.

itinerary.title: Dzongri
itinerary.description: Trek through wooden paths to alpine meadows.

itinerary.title: Thangsing
itinerary.description: Descend into the Prek Chu valley.

itinerary.title: Lamuney
itinerary.description: Short trek to the final camp.

name: Sandakphu Phalut
description: The Sandakphu-Phalut ridge walk (11,941 ft) is the only trek in India from which you can see four of the world's five highest peaks — Everest, Kangchenjunga, Lhotse, and Makalu — simultaneously at dawn. The trail follows the high ridge on the Nepal-India border through forests of magnolia and rhododendron, with traditional Sherpa teahouses serving piping hot chai at every village. A long, rewarding ridge walk that delivers the greatest summit views in the Darjeeling Himalaya.

itinerary.title: Tonglu
itinerary.description: Steep climb to the border of Nepal.

itinerary.title: Sandakphu
itinerary.description: Summit the highest peak in West Bengal.

name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp
description: The Kanchenjunga Base Camp trek is a remote and physically demanding expedition into one of the least-visited corners of the Himalaya — the Kanchenjunga Conservation Area of North Sikkim. The trail passes through sub-tropical forest, yak grazing grounds, and high moraines before reaching the base camp at 17,800 ft below the world's third-highest massif. Limited group permits and restricted access make this a genuine wilderness experience for seasoned high-altitude trekkers.

itinerary.title: KBC
itinerary.description: Push to the final base camp.

name: Dzongri Trail
description: The Dzongri trail (13,215 ft) is the classic acclimatisation route in the Kanchenjunga Conservation Area, climbing through forests of rhododendron to high viewpoints that frame the entire west face of the Kanchenjunga range. The pre-dawn hike to Dzongri Top delivers one of the most spectacular mountain panoramas in Sikkim, with peaks including Kabru, Rathong, and Kokthang glowing in the first light. A perfect introduction to high-altitude Sikkimese trekking.

name: Singalila Ridge
description: The Singalila Ridge trail runs along the Nepal-India border through Singalila National Park, a biodiversity hotspot protecting red pandas, Himalayan black bear, and over 100 species of orchid. The high-altitude ridge walk (11,000–12,000 ft) offers constantly shifting views of the Darjeeling Himalaya and, on clear mornings, the iconic outline of Kanchenjunga dominating the entire western horizon. Rich in both wildlife and scenery, this is one of the finest multi-day treks in Northeast India.

name: Kashmir Great Lakes
description: The Kashmir Great Lakes trek is one of the most visually spectacular multi-day treks in all of South Asia, connecting a chain of seven glacial lakes — Vishansar, Krishansar, Gadsar, Satsar, Gangabal — through passes above 13,500 ft in the Sindh Valley. The high meadows of Kashmir, dotted with wildflowers and flocks of sheep, are as beautiful as anything in the Himalaya. This is a trek where every camp feels like a painting and every morning light a revelation.

itinerary.title: Nichnai
itinerary.description: Steep climb out of the valley.

itinerary.title: Vishansar
itinerary.description: Cross Nichnai Pass to the first lake.

itinerary.title: Gangabal
itinerary.description: Cross Zaj Pass to the twin lakes.

name: Tarsar Marsar
description: The Tarsar Marsar trek connects two impossibly beautiful lakes — Tarsar (13,455 ft) and Marsar (13,648 ft) — through steep passes and glacial meadows above the Aru Valley in Kashmir. The terrain shifts dramatically from dense pine forest to open alpine plateau, and the brilliant blue of the twin lakes set against snowy peaks is one of the finest sights in the entire Himalayan range. A moderately challenging trek through the very heart of the Kashmir wilderness.

itinerary.title: Tarsar
itinerary.description: Arrive at the first pristine lake.

itinerary.title: Sunderdas
itinerary.description: Cross the pass for views of Marsar.

name: Warwan Valley
description: The Warwan Valley trek is one of the most remote and culturally rich trails in Kashmir, crossing the Great Himalayan Range via the Marbog and Bhoj Patri passes to traverse a valley barely touched by modern tourism. The trail passes traditional Gujjar herding camps, ancient nomadic routes, and rivers thick with brown trout, with the soaring walls of the Pir Panjal range looming on every horizon. A genuine off-the-beaten-path adventure for experienced trekkers.

itinerary.title: Sukhnai
itinerary.description: Trek to the isolated wooden village.

name: Naubani Valley
description: The Naubani Valley lies in the lesser-explored region between the Lidder and Sind river catchments of Kashmir, offering a quiet, forested alternative to the more popular tourist trails. The route climbs through meadows carpeted with blue iris and yellow mustard, past glacial streams, and into upper pastures where nomadic Bakkarwal shepherds camp in summer. Ideal for those seeking solitude, wildlife, and the authentic pace of Kashmir's mountain heartland.

name: Tulian Lake
description: Tulian Lake (11,748 ft) is one of the highest accessible lakes in the Kashmir Valley, reached via a demanding ascent from Pahalgam through forests of silver birch and open stony hillside. The lake sits in a natural amphitheatre of rock and scree, partially frozen even in late summer, with the peaks of the Pir Panjal reflected in its steel-grey surface. A rewarding out-and-back trek that packs genuine high-altitude drama into a compact two-day itinerary.

name: Pin Bhaba Pass
description: The Pin Bhaba Pass (16,125 ft) is one of the most accessible high-altitude crossings between the green Kinnaur Valley and the arid Spiti plateau, connecting two of Himachal's most contrasting landscapes in a five-day traverse. The approach through the Bhaba Valley passes dense forests of maple and blue pine before giving way to the sparse, boulder-strewn terrain of the upper snowfield. The descent into Pin Valley is an almost surreal shift from lush Himalaya to Tibetan desert.

name: Mount Kanamo
description: Mount Kanamo (19,574 ft) is the highest trekking peak in Spiti Valley, a non-technical summit accessible to fit trekkers with basic high-altitude experience and strong acclimatisation. The approach through the village of Kibber — one of the world's highest motorable villages — offers views of the entire Spiti valley stretching to the horizon. Standing on Kanamo's summit, surrounded by the brown and ochre folds of the Tibetan plateau, is one of the most uniquely Himalayan experiences available on foot.

name: Chandratal Lake
description: Chandratal — 'Moon Lake' (14,100 ft) — is a crescent-shaped glacial lake of startling turquoise set in the high-altitude desert of Lahaul, accessible via a short but breathtaking trail from the Kunzum Pass road. The lake is a sacred site in both Buddhist and Hindu traditions, and the play of colour across its surface as the light shifts from dawn to dusk is extraordinary. Combining Chandratal with the Hampta Pass crossing creates one of the finest combined treks in the Western Himalaya.

name: Spiti Valley Explore
description: The Spiti Valley Exploration trek follows ancient trade routes through the most dramatic desert-mountain landscape in India, linking mediaeval monasteries, fossil-rich riverbanks, and villages where Tibetan culture survives largely intact at 12,000–14,000 ft. The route threads past the cliff-top gompa of Ki, the painted caves of Tabo, and the wind-carved formations of the Spiti riverbed, offering a journey that is as much a cultural immersion as a physical adventure.

name: Parang La
description: The Parang La trek (19,060 ft) is one of the highest and most challenging crossings in the Indian Himalaya, linking the Spiti Valley with the Rupshu plateau of Ladakh via a multi-day high-altitude traverse above 17,000 ft. The remote landscape of blue sky, brown rock, and glinting ice patches is unlike anything else in India, and the crossing itself demands genuine trekking fitness and experience. A rare and extraordinary journey through one of the last truly wild corridors of the Trans-Himalaya.

itinerary.title: The Pass
itinerary.description: Cross Parang La and walk on the glacier.

itinerary.title: Datang Yongma
itinerary.description: Enter the vast plains of Ladakh.

itinerary.title: Kiangdom
itinerary.description: Reach the southern edge of Tsomoriri.

itinerary.title: Karzok
itinerary.description: Walk along the lake to the village.
